title: Understanding Terminal Velocity
text: Terminal velocity is reached when the force of gravity is balanced by the drag force. At this point, the net force is zero, and the object falls at a constant speed.

title: Formula for Terminal Velocity
text: The formula for terminal velocity \(v_t\) is given by: \(v_t=sqrt((2*m*g)/(C_d*\rho*A))\), where \(m\) is mass, \(g\) is acceleration due to gravity, \(C_d\) is the drag coefficient, \(\rho\) is the density of air, and \(A\) is the cross-sectional area.

title: Calculate the Terminal Velocity
text: Substitute the given values into the formula: $$\(m=80.0\, kg\)$$, \(g=9.81\,m/s**2\), \(C_d=0.70\), \(\rho=1.21\,kg/m**3\), \(A=0.140\,m**2\).
